data.narrative:
Astroscale, a public Space & Aerospace company founded by Mitsunobu Okada, is valued at $840M following $400M+ in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 13 peers across the sector—5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies including Planet Labs, SpaceX, and Apex Space.

Peer-median analysis was the applicable triangulation method. Six same-sector comparables in the similar-valuation-tier range yielded a median valuation of $1.4B, with a range spanning $1.1B (Skyryse, Series C) to $2.3B (Apex Space, Series D). This cohort includes Varda Space Industries at $1.5B (Series C), Cowboy Space Corporation at $2.0B (Series B), and Aalyria at $1.3B (Series B). Public-comp anchoring was not applicable, as Astroscale's revenue is not on file; the sector median P/S of 25.8x across the five public tickers cannot be deployed without disclosed financials.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.2B to $1.6B, with a median indication of $1.4B—representing a 67% premium to current valuation. The primary uncertainty stems from Astroscale's undisclosed revenue and operating metrics relative to peers, limiting precision of revenue-multiple approaches. Stage heterogeneity among comparables (Series B through Series D) introduces moderate variance in the peer distribution.
